features.summary: Microsoft reported strong Q4 FY21 results with revenue of $46.2 billion (up 21%) and EPS of $2.17 (up 49%). Commercial cloud revenue reached $19.5 billion with 36% growth, while commercial bookings grew 30%. Key highlights include Azure revenue growth of 51%, Office 365 Commercial growth of 25%, and Dynamics 365 acceleration to 49% growth. The company saw significant momentum in large enterprise deals with more $10 million-plus contracts across Azure and Microsoft 365. Teams reached nearly 250 million monthly active users, and LinkedIn surpassed $10 billion in annual revenue for the first time.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong demand driven by accelerating digital transformation across all industries and geographies. For Q1 FY22, they guide to Productivity and Business Processes revenue of $14.5-14.75 billion, Intelligent Cloud revenue of $16.4-16.65 billion, and More Personal Computing revenue of $12.4-12.8 billion (including $300 million Windows 11 deferral impact). They anticipate healthy double-digit revenue and operating income growth for FY22 with expanded operating margins when excluding accounting headwinds. Azure growth is expected to remain relatively stable sequentially in constant currency.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing supply chain constraints impacting Windows OEM and Surface revenues, competitive pressures across cloud services, and potential moderation in growth rates as the business scales. The company faces headwinds from a $2.7 billion accounting change for server equipment depreciation and normalization of COVID-related expense savings of $1.2 billion. Foreign exchange volatility and the challenging comparison base from COVID-driven growth in prior periods also present risks to maintaining current growth trajectories.
features.key_drivers: Primary growth drivers include the secular shift to cloud computing with Microsoft positioned across the entire technology stack, hybrid work transformation driving Teams and Microsoft 365 adoption, and AI-first business process automation through Dynamics 365 and Power Platform. The company benefits from increasing enterprise commitments to multi-year Azure contracts, strong momentum in security solutions, and the integration of collaboration tools across business applications. Geographic expansion with 15 new data center regions and edge computing capabilities through 5G deployments also support growth.
